armips WebAssembly vendor notes
===============================

Source:
- Repository: https://github.com/Kingcom/armips
- Commit: 9029577f6448c78dadcffbc876d2b8cbcfc6da9b
- License: MIT, copied in LICENSE.txt

Build toolchain:
- Emscripten 5.0.7 from Homebrew
- CMake + Ninja

Build command:

emcmake cmake -S /tmp/armips-migration -B /tmp/armips-wasm-single-build -GNinja \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DARMIPS_USE_STD_FILESYSTEM=ON \
  -DCMAKE_EXECUTABLE_SUFFIX=.js \
  -DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS='-fexceptions' \
  -DCMAKE_EXE_LINKER_FLAGS='-fexceptions -sMODULARIZE=1 -sEXPORT_NAME=createArmipsModule -sEXPORTED_RUNTIME_METHODS=["callMain","FS","PATH"] -sFORCE_FILESYSTEM=1 -sINVOKE_RUN=0 -sALLOW_MEMORY_GROWTH=1 -sENVIRONMENT=web,node -sWASM=1 -sSINGLE_FILE=1'

cmake --build /tmp/armips-wasm-single-build --target armips-bin -j4

Emscripten compatibility source edits:
- Main/CommandLineInterface.cpp skips fs::absolute(inputFileName) when __EMSCRIPTEN__ is defined.
- Core/Common.cpp keeps FileList relative paths and getFullPathName() relative when __EMSCRIPTEN__ is defined.
- Core/Assembler.cpp sets Global.multiThreading = false when __EMSCRIPTEN__ is defined.

Runtime note:
- vendor/armips/armips.js is the single-file app runtime so index.html can still be opened directly from disk.
- vendor/armips/armips.wasm is retained as the split build artifact from the same source/toolchain for inspection and rebuild comparison.
